topic: How Claude Code integrates new LSP plugins, and the contribution path for adding one (specifically: a Zig / zls LSP plugin)

sources.title: Plugins Reference — Claude Code Docs
keyInsights: Full .lsp.json schema with all fields and types
Plugin directory structure reference
All optional LSP fields: args, transport, env, initializationOptions, settings, workspaceFolder, startupTimeout, shutdownTimeout, restartOnCrash, maxRestarts
Plugin manifest schema (plugin.json) all fields
CLI commands reference: plugin install/uninstall/enable/disable/validate
Debug commands: claude --debug, /plugin Errors tab, claude plugin validate

sources.title: Create Plugins — Claude Code Docs
keyInsights: Plugin directory structure with .claude-plugin/ containing only plugin.json
.lsp.json goes at plugin root, not inside .claude-plugin/
Test with --plugin-dir flag: claude --plugin-dir ./my-plugin
Submit to official marketplace via claude.ai/settings/plugins/submit or platform.claude.com/plugins/submit
/reload-plugins command for hot-reload during development
LSP plugins belong in plugins/ for official Anthropic or external_plugins/ for third-party

sources.title: Discover and Install Plugins — Claude Code Docs
keyInsights: Official marketplace auto-available as claude-plugins-official
All 11 official LSP plugins listed with binary requirements
ruby-lsp also appears in directory listing (12th plugin?)
Plugin install scopes: user, project, local, managed
/plugin marketplace add command syntax for github, git URL, local path, remote URL
LSP diagnostic hook behavior: automatic after every file edit, no contributor wiring needed

sources.title: Create and Distribute a Plugin Marketplace — Claude Code Docs
keyInsights: marketplace.json schema: name, owner, plugins array, metadata.pluginRoot
Plugin entry required fields: name, source
Source types: local path (./), github (owner/repo), git URL, npm
Reserved marketplace names list
strict: false in marketplace entry allows inline lspServers without a plugin directory — but this is the pattern that caused Issue #379
Version management: explicit version in plugin.json vs git commit SHA

sources.title: anthropics/claude-plugins-official — Official Plugin Directory
keyInsights: Repository structure: plugins/ (Anthropic-internal), external_plugins/ (third-party)
33 plugins total in plugins/ directory including 11 LSP plugins + ruby-lsp
Submission via form at https://clau.de/plugin-directory-submission
Quality and security review required before approval
No open PR-based contribution — form-gated process
Each plugin requires .claude-plugin/plugin.json, .lsp.json at root, README.md

sources.title: PR #378 — Add .lsp.json files to all LSP plugin directories
keyInsights: All 11 official LSP plugins were missing .lsp.json — only had README.md
lspServers in marketplace.json is NOT copied to plugin cache — must be in plugin directory
Exact .lsp.json content for all 11 official plugins revealed
jdtls and kotlin have startupTimeout: 120000 (JVM startup)
gopls and rust-analyzer have no startupTimeout (fast native binaries)
pyright and typescript use args: ['--stdio']
clangd uses args: ['--background-index']

sources.title: Issue #379 — All LSP plugins missing .lsp.json
keyInsights: Affects every user of any official LSP plugin
Root cause: installer copies only source directory, not marketplace.json inline config
Multiple related issues: claude-code#15235, #16291, #15148, #14803
PR #378 is the fix — add physical .lsp.json to each plugin directory
Critical learning: never rely on lspServers in marketplace.json alone

sources.title: Issue #14803 — LSP plugins not recognized despite correct config
keyInsights: Symptom: No LSP server available for file type even with correct plugin
Debug: check --debug output for Total LSP servers loaded: N
--enable-lsp-logging flag; logs at ~/.claude/debug/lsp-*.log
Expected cache structure: ~/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/
Community workaround: Piebald-AI/claude-code-lsps with tweakcc patch
Issue is closed — fixed in later Claude Code versions

sources.title: cc-zig-lsp — Community Zig LSP Plugin for Claude Code
keyInsights: Complete .lsp.json: command: zls, .zig/.zon to zig, initializationOptions with enable_snippets/enable_import_analysis, maxRestarts: 3
plugin.json structure with lspServers pointer
Auto-install hooks: downloads zls if not found in PATH
Version detection from build.zig.zon, .zigversion, zig version command
ZLS cache at ~/.local/share/zls/, symlink at ~/.local/bin/zls
Platform support: macOS and Linux (x86_64, aarch64) — no Windows

sources.title: Piebald-AI/claude-code-lsps — Community LSP Marketplace
keyInsights: 40+ language server plugins, no Zig/ZLS included (contribution opportunity)
.lsp.json is canonical source; marketplace.json lspServers generated from it
Do not hand-edit generated lspServers blocks
Validation scripts sync .lsp.json to marketplace.json
Targets Claude Code 2.1.50+
Correct pattern: .lsp.json at plugin root, marketplace.json auto-generated

sources.title: zigtools/zls — Zig Language Server
keyInsights: Binary name: zls (zls.exe on Windows)
Supported: completions, hover, goto definition/declaration, document symbols, find references, rename, formatting (zig fmt), semantic tokens, inlay hints, code actions, selection ranges, folding
Limitation: comptime analysis is Work-in-Progress
Configuration via schema.json — delivered in initializationOptions
Must match Zig version exactly

sources.title: ZLS Installation Guide — zigtools
keyInsights: Prebuilt binaries are signed with minisign key RWR+9B91GBZ0zOjh6Lr17+zKf5BoSuFvrx2xSeDE57uIYvnKBGmMjOex
Tagged releases must pair with matching ZLS tagged releases
Installation tool resolves compatible versions automatically
VS Code extension can manage both Zig and ZLS install — most reliable method

sources.title: ZLS schema.json — All Configuration Keys
keyInsights: enable_snippets (bool, true): enables snippet completions
enable_argument_placeholders (bool, true): function argument placeholder completions
enable_build_on_save (bool, null): enables build-on-save diagnostics
build_on_save_args (array, []): args for zig build during save
semantic_tokens (enum: none/partial/full, full): level of semantic tokens
inlay_hints_show_variable_type_hints (bool, true)
inlay_hints_show_parameter_name (bool, true)
prefer_ast_check_as_child_process (bool, true): use zig ast-check
zig_exe_path (string, null): override zig executable path
zig_lib_path (string, null): override zig library path
warn_style (bool, false): style guideline warnings
force_autofix (bool, false): workaround for editors lacking source.fixall

sources.title: Improving Your ZLS Experience — Loris Cro
keyInsights: enable_build_on_save + build_on_save_step: check is the key config for compilation errors
build_on_save step in build.zig: addExecutable without installArtifact triggers -fno-emit-bin, skips LLVM, fast diagnostics
Without build_on_save, only parser/syntax errors, not type errors
zls --show-config-path reveals config file location
Config file location: Linux ~/.config/zls.json, macOS Application Support folder
Restart editor AND zls after config changes

codeExamples.description: build.zig check step for fast build-on-save

sources.title: Using Claude Code LSP Without the Official Marketplace
keyInsights: Complete local marketplace workflow without official marketplace
strict: false in marketplace.json allows defining plugins inline (but avoid — see Issue #379)
source field must point to a valid directory even with strict: false
JSON Schema 404 on validation — ignore this error
Enable LSP in .claude/settings.json if needed
/plugin marketplace add ./.your-claude-marketplace syntax

sources.title: claude-code/plugins README
keyInsights: Demo marketplace (claude-code-plugins) separate from official marketplace
Add with: /plugin marketplace add anthropics/claude-code
Demo plugins include: commit-commands, code-review, feature-dev, etc.
These are examples of non-LSP plugins for reference

sources.title: claude-plugins-official marketplace.json
keyInsights: gopls-lsp marketplace entry: source ./plugins/gopls-lsp, strict: false, lspServers inline
Schema: anthropic.com/claude-code/marketplace.schema.json
Official marketplace name: claude-plugins-official
1932 lines, ~81 KB — large file with 100+ external plugins

sources.title: zircote/lsp-marketplace — 28 LSP-enabled Claude Code Plugins
keyInsights: 28 LSP plugins available in community marketplace
Includes automated code quality hooks
Alternative to official marketplace for LSP needs

sources.title: claude-code-lsps CLAUDE.md — Project Guidelines
keyInsights: .lsp.json is authoritative source for LSP config in this marketplace
marketplace.json lspServers sections are generated, not hand-edited
Validation scripts ensure consistency
plugin.json, .lsp.json are required per plugin
startupTimeout used for slow servers

sources.title: cc-zig-lsp plugin.json
keyInsights: name: zig-lsp
lspServers: ./.lsp.json pointer pattern
keywords: zig, lsp, zls, language-server
version: 0.1.0
repository field for discoverability

sources.title: cc-zig-lsp .lsp.json
keyInsights: command: zls
extensions: .zig and .zon both map to zig language
initializationOptions: enable_snippets, enable_import_analysis
maxRestarts: 3
No startupTimeout (fast binary)

sources.title: How to Build Claude Code Plugins — DataCamp
keyInsights: Step-by-step plugin creation walkthrough
Confirms --plugin-dir for local testing
Confirms /reload-plugins for hot-reload

sources.title: ZLS Setup Discussion #1715
keyInsights: Binary name is zls.exe on Windows, zls everywhere else
PATH setup: add zls directory to system PATH
Common issue: command not found when PATH not set
VS Code extension is most reliable install method

sources.title: PR #378 Files — Authoritative .lsp.json Content for All 11 Official LSP Plugins
keyInsights: clangd: args --background-index, 9 file extensions
csharp-ls: minimal, no args
gopls: minimal, no args, no startupTimeout
jdtls: startupTimeout 120000
kotlin-lsp: args --stdio, startupTimeout 120000
lua-language-server: command lua-language-server
intelephense: args --stdio
pyright-langserver: args --stdio, .py and .pyi
rust-analyzer: minimal, no args, no timeout
sourcekit-lsp: minimal
typescript-language-server: args --stdio, 8 file extensions
